reason: The statement does apply to the text fragment as it depicts a situation where the righteous prophet Elisha, under Yahweh's guidance, delivers a message of salvation to the people of Samaria by predicting the end of a famine, which ultimately comes true. This act of protection and deliverance of the people from suffering can be seen as Yahweh protecting the righteous and punishing the wicked by causing the downfall of the Syrian army who had besieged Samaria.
